pr.title: Local Runner: Windows-safe module invocation to bypass WDAC exe-shim block (WinError 4551)
pr.description:
## Summary

Windows lane-2 revalidation found the Local Runner's allowlisted actions fail on Plamen's machine with `[WinError 4551] An Application Control policy has blocked this file` — Windows Application Control (WDAC / Device Guard) blocks the console-script `.exe` shims (`poetry.exe`, `ruff.exe`, `pytest.exe`) that the runner spawns. Direct **module** invocation (`python -m poetry ...`, `python -m ruff/pytest`) runs identical code without hitting the shim. This PR makes the runner emit that module form automatically, **on Windows only**.

**Approach:** a pure argv transform applied at execution time. No new arguments, no new action surface — the allowlist stays the security boundary.

```python
# execution loop (tools/local_runner/local_runner.py)
for cmd in commands:
    cmd = _windows_safe_command(cmd)   # no-op unless os.name == "nt"
    executed_commands.append(cmd)
    subprocess.run(cmd, ...)

# _windows_safe_command, Windows only:
#   ["poetry", "run", "ruff",  ...]  -> [sys.executable, "-m", "poetry", "run", "python", "-m", "ruff",  ...]
#   ["poetry", "run", "pytest",...]  -> [sys.executable, "-m", "poetry", "run", "python", "-m", "pytest",...]
#   ["poetry", "run", "axiom", ...]  -> [sys.executable, "-m", "poetry", "run", "axiom", ...]
#   ["poetry", "install"]            -> [sys.executable, "-m", "poetry", "install"]
#   ["git", "status", ...]           -> unchanged (non-poetry heads untouched)
```

The inner interpreter stays the bare string `"python"` so `poetry run` resolves the project venv's interpreter (matches the forms Plamen proved work). `command_display` is updated post-loop to reflect what actually ran, so run logs stay honest.

**What did not change:** task.json schema, action names, the allowlist, workspace/trusted-root policy, and **all Linux/CI behavior** (the transform is a no-op off Windows, so `poetry`/`ruff` resolve exactly as before — `python -m poetry` may not even exist in CI).

**Tests** (`tests/test_local_runner.py`, new `TestWindowsSafeCommand`): per-rule normalization (leading poetry, `run ruff`, `run pytest`, `run axiom`, non-poetry untouched, no-op off Windows / on empty); a **simulated-Windows guarantee** that iterates every allowlisted action and asserts no bare `poetry`/`ruff`/`pytest` shim head survives; and an execution-path test asserting the rewritten argv is what reaches `subprocess.run`. `61 passed, 1 skipped`; ruff clean.

**Docs:** WDAC `WinError 4551` symptom + `python -m poetry ...` direct-CLI fallback added to `windows-post-151-revalidation-runbook.md` (incl. a note that PowerShell `>` writes UTF-16, which is what broke the operator's JSON count commands — an encoding artifact, not empty data), and a Windows-safe-invocation section in `local-runner-runbook.md`.

**Factory relevance:** unblocks the Local Runner as the loop-execution substrate on the real target environment (lane 2).

## Validation still pending / not claimed
- **Windows lane-2 is NOT validated by this PR.** It must be confirmed by Plamen re-running the runner actions (`git_status`, `ruff`, `execution_chain_run`, `capability_evidence_apply`) on `C:\Dev\Axiom\Code\Axiom-platform` — the fix is verified here only via Linux unit tests + simulated-Windows assertions.
- Does not touch Revit; 2024 baseline unaffected; no Revit-live validation required.
- **Separate, still-open item:** the semantic evidence-quality gate (Finding 2 — chain `PASS` with `module_count=0` promoted to `very_high`) is intentionally NOT in this PR; design diff pending approval.

## Known risks
- Low. If `python -m poetry` is somehow unavailable on a given Windows box, that action would fail — but that path only activates on Windows, where the operator already confirmed `python -m poetry` works.


Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/8ad1c9ac0d2f4252bb77647f1eabaac7
Requested by: @plamen-dev
